title: Rhino Bridge - Open App
meta_description: rhino bridge opens the Rhino.fi app for supported cross-chain transfers; review networks, quotes, and wallet prompts before signing.
hero_lead: rhino bridge opens Rhino.fi's official app for moving USDC, USDT, ETH, WBTC and other supported assets across available networks with a route quote before you sign.
what_is: Rhino.fi is stablecoin deposits infrastructure and an app destination for routing supported assets between networks. Use the app to review a quote, confirm the route, and complete the transaction with your wallet.

how.d: Go to the official Rhino.fi app and confirm the domain before connecting a wallet.
Select the token, source network, destination network, and recipient details.
Check the route, expected destination asset, gas, and any app-displayed fees.
Approve only the wallet prompts you understand, then track the transfer from the app.

points.p: The landing page should route users directly to app.rhino.fi, where the wallet connection and transaction signing happen.
Confirm source assets and destination networks in Rhino.fi's supported chains page before starting a transfer.
Rhino.fi documents both same-token bridging and bridge + swap routes, so the final asset can depend on the supported path you select.

faq.a: rhino bridge is the search intent for opening Rhino.fi's cross-chain app experience, where users can route supported assets between available networks after reviewing a quote.

faq.q: Is rhino bridge the official Rhino.fi app?
faq.a: This landing page is not the official app; the transaction flow should happen on app.rhino.fi. Always verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: Which assets can I use with rhino bridge?
faq.a: Rhino.fi documentation lists support for assets such as USDT, USDC, ETH, WBTC and selected stablecoins across specific chains; availability depends on the route.

faq.q: Does rhino bridge support swaps as well as transfers?
faq.a: Rhino.fi docs describe Bridge and Bridge + swap route types, meaning a supported path may move the same token across chains or convert into a supported destination token.

Any cross-chain transfer carries smart contract, routing, wallet, and user-error risk; Ethereum.org's bridge overview explains general bridge risks. Check the domain, route, recipient, and wallet prompt before signing.

footer_note: This is not the official app; verify app.rhino.fi before connecting a wallet. Educational information only, not financial advice.
